replication_study.hypothesis: German women (coded female = 1) will report a higher mean level of general worry about COVID-19 (mh_anxiety_1, 5-point Likert scale) than German men (female = 0).

data_plan.source_type: Online access-panel survey (same questionnaire items as original study)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Full 10,192-case release available on GitHub; not restricted to the 19–23 March 2020 fieldwork that produced the 1,242 cases analysed in Gerhold (2020).
data_plan.sample_size: 10,192 observations; after dropping respondents with gender == 3, n ≈ 10,071.
data_plan.access_details: Open CSV file bundled in replication_data folder; originally hosted on https://github.com/jamiefield/…/data_gerhold.csv (no restrictions).

qualification.explanation: The dataset contains the identical questionnaire items (mh_anxiety_1 for COVID-19 worry, gender indicator, etc.) that were used to test the focal gender–worry difference, therefore allowing a direct statistical replication while providing an independent sample.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Same survey instrument (see identical item wording in original_paper.pdf p. 5, Table 4, and variables mh_anxiety_1 & gender in data_gerhold.csv). Same country (CountryofLiving = Germany for all 10,192 rows). Same 5-point Likert coding (1 = strongly disagree … 5 = strongly agree).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Larger and later sample (10,192 vs 1,242 respondents; post_registration.json reports original field period 19–23 March 2020, whereas the GitHub file aggregates additional respondents collected after that wave—exact dates not in file). Weight variables (weight_sample, weight_new) were added, which were not used in the original analysis.

data_plan.notes: Variables: mh_anxiety_1 (worry), mh_anxiety_3 (fear of infection), gender (1=female,2=male,3=other), pre-computed dummy female, and two weight variables. No explicit interview date column.

planned_method.steps:
1. Load data_gerhold.csv from /app/data.
2. Exclude respondents with gender == 3 ("other / missing").
3. Keep analytic variables: female (0/1) and mh_anxiety_1.
4. Inspect group variances with Fisher’s F-test (var.test).
5. If homoscedastic, run two-sample Student t-test; else Welch t-test.
6. Compute Cohen’s d effect size.
7. (Optional) replicate on mh_anxiety_3 for exploratory check.
8. Compare direction, effect size and p-value with original study.
planned_method.tools_software: R (>=4.0), packages: readr, dplyr, psych, effsize (for Cohen’s d).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Difference in group means (female – male) and Cohen’s d.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion of cases with gender == 3 or NA on mh_anxiety_1.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replication judged successful if (a) mean_female > mean_male and (b) p < .05.

files.Gerhold_covid_Azg9_0948_final.R: End-to-end R script that: (1) reads data_gerhold.csv, (2) drops gender == 3, (3) produces descriptive stats, (4) performs F-test and t-test on mh_anxiety_1, (5) repeats for mh_anxiety_3, (6) outputs Cohen’s d. All paths already point to /app/data via the local copy of the CSV.

codebase.notes: Script relies only on CRAN packages. No edits needed except ensuring working directory points to /app/data if additional scripts are added.

analysis.instructions: Inside the container, run Rscript /app/data/replication_data/Gerhold_covid_Azg9_0948_final.R (or an updated script) to reproduce statistics. Ensure working directory has access to /app/data.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Direction of mean difference, p-value (<.05), and Cohen’s d compared with original study’s reported p < .01 and positive gender effect.
